metadata.title:
MARKET


0vERRUN

BIG INFLUX OF OUTSIDE
WORklNGMEN WILL CAUSE
A RUSH TO THE HARVEST
FIELD.

pages.raw_text:
From present indications many
of Spokane's workingmen will go
to the wehat fields for employment
during harvest this fall. Seattle is
filled to overlooking with men
looking for work, and there is now
little chance of getting jobs there
for any of the outside idle men,
many of whom have come to Spo-
kane.


Even the bakers are having a
hard time keeping in work. In the
small towns many of the bakers
were turned off when the price of
flour began to go up by leaps and
bounds. The proprietors or boss
bakers took their places and things
are coming back to their normal
condition very slowly.


A great many working men from
the east who flocked in to take
their chances of getting work at
the Seattle exposition are now in
Spokane looking for something to
do. Among the unskilled and man.
ual laborers present indications
promise a plenty of work for the
rest of the season. 1t is the men
with trades that are now out of
work. Harvest will soon begin in
the whole surrounding country and
it is believed that the highest
wages paid in years will be OF
fered for competent help in the
grain fields and with threshing out
fits. At the present time farmers
are said to be short of help.
